meta.name: Microsoft AI Business Professional (AB-730)
meta.description: Business-focused AI concepts - identifying use cases, applying Microsoft Copilot, improving workflows, and driving organizational productivity with AI.

cards.definition: Technology that enables machines to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as reasoning, learning, and decision-making. In AB-730, the focus is on applying AI to solve business problems.
A type of AI that creates new content such as text, images, or code based on patterns learned from data. Used in Copilot to generate emails, documents, and insights.
cards.hint: AI that creates content, not just analyzes it.

cards.definition: An AI assistant integrated into Microsoft 365 apps that helps users draft content, analyze data, and automate tasks using natural language.
A specific scenario where AI can be applied to solve a problem or improve a process, such as automating customer support or generating reports.
The process of selecting AI use cases based on business impact, feasibility, and return on investment.
cards.hint: Pick the most valuable AI opportunities first.

cards.definition: A measure of the value gained from AI adoption compared to its cost, often evaluated through productivity gains and cost savings.
Efficiency improvements achieved by using AI tools to reduce manual effort and speed up workflows.
cards.hint: Doing more work in less time.

cards.definition: Using AI and tools like Power Automate to reduce manual tasks and streamline business processes.
Helps generate, edit, and summarize documents, improving writing efficiency.
Analyzes data, identifies trends, and generates insights without requiring complex formulas.
Provides meeting summaries, highlights decisions, and tracks action items.
A Copilot feature that aggregates data across Microsoft 365 to answer questions and assist with tasks.
Crafting effective instructions to guide AI outputs toward desired results.
A model where humans review and validate AI outputs before taking action.
When an AI system generates incorrect or fabricated information.
The practice of designing AI systems that are ethical, fair, and trustworthy.
Ensuring AI systems do not produce biased or discriminatory outcomes.
Providing visibility into how AI systems make decisions.
Ensuring humans remain responsible for AI outcomes.
A structured approach to transitioning individuals and teams to adopt AI tools effectively.
A plan to ensure successful implementation and usage of AI across an organization.
Ensuring all key stakeholders agree on AI goals and implementation plans.
Improving workflows using AI to increase efficiency and reduce costs.
Using data and AI insights to guide business decisions.
Platforms like Power Apps and Copilot Studio that allow building solutions without deep coding knowledge.
A Microsoft tool for automating workflows and integrating systems.
A platform for building custom business applications with minimal coding.
A platform for creating custom AI agents and copilots tailored to business needs.
AI systems that can perform tasks autonomously and interact with users or systems.
The overall experience a customer has with a company, often improved through AI automation and personalization.
cards.hint: How customers feel about your service.

cards.definition: Using AI to tailor experiences and content to individual users.
Organizing and using company knowledge effectively, often enhanced with AI search and summarization.
Protecting personal and sensitive data when using AI systems.
Ensuring AI usage meets legal and regulatory requirements.
Managing data availability, usability, and security across an organization.
Ensuring AI systems respect existing access permissions and data restrictions.
The ability of AI solutions to grow and handle increased demand.
cards.hint: Can it grow with the business?

cards.definition: Connecting AI tools with existing business systems and workflows.
cards.hint: Make AI work with your systems.

cards.definition: Monitoring and controlling expenses associated with AI solutions.
Ensuring AI investments deliver measurable business outcomes.
Educating users on how to effectively use AI tools.
Testing AI solutions with a small group before full deployment.
Collecting user feedback to improve AI solutions over time.
Identifying and mitigating risks associated with AI adoption.
Using AI in a way that aligns with moral values and societal norms.
The accuracy and reliability of data used in AI systems.
AI tools that assist humans in making informed decisions.
Automation replaces tasks with AI, while augmentation enhances human capabilities.
Using AI to create new products, services, or business models.
cards.hint: AI as a driver of innovation.

cards.definition: A long-term plan for adopting and scaling AI across an organization to achieve business goals.
cards.hint: The big picture for AI adoption.
